sentences.en: Saturday, New Year's Eve. Sophia has been planning tonight's dinner for at least two weeks, checking her calendar every single day.
sentences.zh: 星期六，除夕夜。Sophia 至少提前兩個星期就開始籌劃今晚的年夜飯，每天都在看她的日曆確認日期。

sentences.en: "This is not just any dinner," she reminded us for the tenth time. "This is the one meal all year that must be grand."
sentences.zh: 「這不只是普通的一餐，」她已經第十次提醒我們了：「這是全年當中唯一一餐必須辦得盛大的晚飯。」

sentences.en: The whole extended family gathered at our house: aunts, uncles, a few cousins, and one very newly married couple everyone kept congratulating.
sentences.zh: 整個大家庭都聚集到我們家：阿姨、叔叔、幾個表親，還有一對剛結婚的新婚夫妻，每個人都不停向他們道賀。

sentences.en: "Congratulations on your marriage," Sophia said warmly, greeting them at the door before they had even fully stepped inside.
sentences.zh: 「恭喜你們結婚，」Sophia 熱情地說，他們都還沒完全踏進門，她就在門口迎接他們了。

sentences.en: Before dinner, Sophia lit a candle near the little shelf and said a quiet prayer, the same one she says every year without fail.
sentences.zh: 晚餐前，Sophia 在小神桌旁點了一根蠟燭，唸了一段安靜的禱詞，每年都是同一段，從不間斷。

sentences.en: "This tradition's origin goes back further than even I can remember," she said. "My own grandmother did the exact same thing."
sentences.zh: 「這個傳統的起源，連我自己都追溯不到，」她說：「我自己的阿嬤當年也是做一模一樣的事。」

sentences.en: William asked why we do the same thing every single year, and Sophia said some things are only meaningful because they repeat, like the cycle of the seasons themselves.
sentences.zh: William 問我們為什麼每年都要做一樣的事，Sophia 說有些事情之所以有意義，正是因為它們會重複，就像四季本身的循環一樣。

sentences.en: Dinner was enormous: an entire table covered in dishes, each one apparently symbolizing luck, growth, or good fortune for the coming year.
sentences.zh: 晚餐份量驚人：一整桌都是菜，每一道菜顯然都象徵著來年的好運、成長，或是好福氣。

sentences.en: After dinner, the whole family gathered around the television to watch a small stage performance broadcast live from downtown, full of lion dances and drums.
sentences.zh: 晚餐後，全家人聚在電視前，觀看從市區現場直播的一場小型舞台表演，滿滿的舞獅和鼓聲。

sentences.en: The newly married couple shared stories about their own small wedding, and Sophia greeted every detail with approving nods.
sentences.zh: 那對新婚夫妻分享了他們自己小型婚禮的故事，Sophia 對每個細節都點頭表示贊同。

sentences.en: William fell asleep on the couch before midnight, still holding half of a candy he was supposed to be saving for good luck.
sentences.zh: William 在午夜之前就在沙發上睡著了，手裡還握著半顆本來應該留著討吉利的糖果。

sentences.en: One by one, relatives began saying their goodbyes, promising to gather again soon, the same promise made every single year.
sentences.zh: 親戚們一個接一個開始道別，答應很快會再聚一次，這是每年都會許下的同一個承諺。

sentences.en: Sophia stood at the door greeting each departing guest personally, the same way she had welcomed them hours earlier.
sentences.zh: Sophia 站在門口，親自向每位離開的客人道別，就跟幾個小時前歡迎他們進門時一樣。

sentences.en: By the time the house was quiet again, it was well past midnight, and a brand-new year had already quietly begun.
sentences.zh: 等家裡再次安靜下來的時候，早已過了午夜，全新的一年已經悄悄開始了。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: some traditions do not need a clear reason to matter, they just need a family willing to keep repeating them.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：有些傳統不需要明確的理由才有意義，它們只需要一個願意持續傳承下去的家庭。

quiz.question: What does Sophia do before dinner, near the little shelf?
options: Lights a candle and says a quiet prayer.
Calls all the relatives on the phone.

quiz.question: According to Sophia, why do some traditions matter even though they repeat every year?
options: Some things are only meaningful because they repeat, like the cycle of the seasons.
They only matter to older people.
They are meant to be different each time.

quiz.question: Who does everyone congratulate at the family dinner?
options: A cousin who got a new job.
